rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RODNEY BRUCE MIDDLETON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/6/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proof of service of the amended petition has been filed. A proof of service filed on 4/1/22 shows that notice was mailed on 4/1/22 -- before the pleading was filed. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The amended petition was file stamped on 4/4/22 but not efiled (and available for viewing) until 4/5/22. In either case, those dates were not at least 15 days before this hearing. Exhibit E is the calculation of the statutory fee, not expenses and costs, as stated in paragraph 34. Exhibit G still shows the incorrect calculation of the statutory compensation. Exhibit I is the legal description of the property on Prosper Street, not a Notice of Partial Assignment, as stated in paragraph 40. There is no Exhibit J, as mentioned in subparagraph b of paragraph 5 of the prayer. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/26/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KAR WAI LEUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Succession T Personal Property
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The decedent's former spouse residing in Hong Kong has not been served with a copy of this petition and notice of the hearing in the manner required by Probate Code section 851(a)(2) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. applicable to service of a summons in a civil action. The proof of service on file does not show service on the former spouse in Hong Kong was effected in a manner authorized under the Hague Service Convention to which Hong Kong is a party. See CCP section 413.10(c). 2) The stipulated divorce judgment did not divide the decedent's Roth IRA as community property, nor did the judgment confirm the Roth IRA as the decedent's separate property. It is unclear whether the decedent's former spouse had any property interest in the Roth IRA at the time of their divorce. No postjudgment motion appears to have been filed by the petitioner as personal representative of the estate of the deceased spouse in the divorce proceeding to seek an order from the family court determining the property characterization and dividing the Roth IRA as an omitted asset under Family Code section 2556. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGARET MARY EIMIL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petitioner, who is a beneficiary named in the decedent's 1985 will sought to be admitted to probate, alleges that he "first obtained knowledge" of the decedent's will on 12/18/2021 when the current personal representative, who is also a beneficiary named in the will, disclosed to the petitioner that the will had been found. The current personal representative first obtained knowledge of the will in October 2021 and did not petition for probate of the will within the 60-day limitation period under Probate Code section 8226(c)(2). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LOUIS GENATO PERSONAL INJURY SETTLEMENT TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 16, 2003
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Modify Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Neither trustee's residence or usual place of business is in San Francisco County. The Court is to determine whether venue is proper in San Francisco County. Probate Code sections 17002 & 17005(a)(1). 2) Louis Genato, the current sole beneficiary of the trust, has not consented to the proposed modification and has no capacity to consent. No conservator of the estate or guardian ad litem has been appointed for Louis Genato. 3) The Court is to determine whether a guardian ad litem should be appointed to represent unascertained heirs of Louis Genato as beneficiaries of the trust whose consent is required to modify the trust, or whether the heirs whose consent is required for the proposed modification should be limited. Sections 15403(c) & 15405. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCINE MARIE DOUGLASS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/16/2022 again for same issues. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Monica A. Douglass has priority to serve as administrator under Probate Code 8461. Please file a declination to serve/nomination from Monica A. Douglass. 2) Notice did not include the language required by the 10th Amended General Order for the Probate and Mental Health Division found on the court's website. Please re-notice with the required language. 3) Please file Duties and Liabilities judicial council form DE-147. 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 5) Please file proof of publication. 6) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/9/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HOWARD A. COOPER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered, regardless of what the testator stated in his will. If the decedent was survived by issue of a predeceased child, the petition must be supplemented with their names and addresses, and the hearing must be continued for notice to be given to them. If the petitioner appears and swears that the decedent was not survived by issue of a predeceased child, the Court might wish to proceed with the hearing. 2) The petitioner's address in item 8 of the petition differs from the address shown in the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities. The petitioner should clarify the discrepancy. 3) An amendment requires the same notice as the pleading it amends (including publication). [CRC 7.53(a).] Since the amendment filed on 4/4/22 was merely to correctly identify the newspaper in which notice was published, the Court might wish to waive notice of the amendment. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.
